county_metadata.record_losses: No significant record losses. Records well preserved.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Ontario County in 1802. Originally vast territory; many western NY counties carved from it. Holland Land Company headquarters in Batavia. Agricultural heartland.
county_metadata.research_tips: Genesee County Clerk in Batavia holds deed records from 1802 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Critically important: the Holland Land Company records (1792-1846) were headquartered in Batavia and these land sale records are essential for tracing early settlers in all of western NY. The Holland Land Office Museum in Batavia has exhibits and reference materials. Original Holland Land Company records are at the Erie County Clerk and NYS Archives. Pre-1802 records in Ontario County. The Genesee County History Department maintains cemetery transcriptions and genealogical files. The Richmond Memorial Library in Batavia has local history collections. Genesee County was much larger at formation and many early records relate to areas now in other counties (Erie, Niagara, Allegany, Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Wyoming, Orleans, Livingston).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1802; Holland Land Company records from 1792

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Holland Land Company deeds critical.
Wills, administrations from 1802.

source_profiles.repository: Holland Land Office Museum / NYS Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Land sales for all western NY. Essential for tracing early settlers across multiple counties.
Federal census from 1810. Early censuses cover territory later in other counties.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Batavia Republican, Batavia Daily News.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Albany and Ulster Counties in 1800. Catskill Mountains and Hudson River. Thomas Cole founded Hudson River School of painting here. Catskill was a major steamboat landing.
county_metadata.research_tips: Greene County Clerk in Catskill holds deed records from 1800 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1800 records split between Albany and Ulster Counties by town location. The Greene County Historical Society (Vedder Research Library) in Coxsackie has excellent genealogical collections including family files, Bible records, and cemetery transcriptions. The Thomas Cole National Historic Site in Catskill connects to the artistic community. Dutch settlement along the Hudson in Coxsackie, Athens, and Catskill means Dutch Reformed church records are essential — particularly Coxsackie Reformed (records from 1730s). The Catskill Mountain resorts (boarding houses) generated seasonal worker records. Tannersville and other mountain communities had tanning industry records. Prattsville was a model industrial town founded by Zadock Pratt with well-documented records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1800; county clerk Catskill

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses, though records are sparse due to tiny population.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Montgomery County in 1816. Adirondack wilderness county, least populated in NY. Lake Pleasant is county seat. Entirely within Adirondack Park.
county_metadata.research_tips: Hamilton County Clerk in Lake Pleasant holds deed records from 1816 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1816 records in Montgomery County. Hamilton is the least populated county in NY (under 5,000 residents) so records are sparse but well-preserved. The county is entirely within the Adirondack Park and most land is state-owned forest preserve. The Hamilton County Historical Society maintains the few genealogical files that exist. Most families were involved in logging, hunting/trapping, and tourism. Adirondack Museum (now The Wild Center) in Tupper Lake documents regional life. Great camp records (wealthy families' Adirondack estates) are scattered across various repositories. Lumber company records document seasonal and permanent workers. Due to tiny population, most researchers will find only a few generations here before families link to Montgomery or surrounding counties.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1816; very small volume due to low population

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Small volume.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Limited coverage due to few publications.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Montgomery County in 1791. Named for Revolutionary War hero Nicholas Herkimer. Mohawk Valley location. Erie Canal passed through. Herkimer diamonds (quartz crystals). Cheese-making region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Herkimer County Clerk holds deed records from 1791 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1791 records in Montgomery County (formerly Tryon). The Mohawk Valley location makes German Palatine settlement records critical — many families arrived in the 1720s-1750s. The Herkimer County Historical Society in Herkimer has genealogical files and local histories. Ilion was home to Remington Arms factory (from 1816) generating extensive employment records. Little Falls Historical Society has canal-era records. The Erie Canal (1825) transformed the county and canal worker records are at NYS Archives. For German Flatts area research, the Reformed Protestant Dutch Church of German Flatts records (from 1728) are essential. Herkimer Home State Historic Site has Nicholas Herkimer family records. Frank Woolworth started his five-and-dime in Utica-Rome area nearby.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1791; county clerk Herkimer

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1791.
Reformed church records from German Flatts area. Baptisms, marriages critical for Palatine German families.
Federal census from 1800. Manufacturing schedules for Remington Arms.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Oneida County in 1805. Northern NY on Lake Ontario and St. Lawrence River. Fort Drum military base. Thousand Islands region. Watertown is county seat.
county_metadata.research_tips: Jefferson County Clerk in Watertown holds deed records from 1805 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1805 records in Oneida County. Fort Drum (originally Pine Camp, established 1908) military base records are at NARA. The Jefferson County Historical Society in Watertown has extensive genealogical files and military records. The Thousand Islands region attracted wealthy families whose estate records are valuable. Sackets Harbor was a major War of 1812 naval base and battle records document local participants. The Flower Memorial Library in Watertown has local history collections. French Canadian immigration was significant — check Diocese of Ogdensburg Catholic records. The county's paper mill industry (Champion International, others) generated employment records. Cape Vincent was a French Royalist colony (1815-1830) with unique settlement records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1805; county clerk Watertown

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant courthouse fires. Records well preserved from colonial period.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Coterminous with Borough of Brooklyn (consolidated into NYC 1898). Was independent City of Brooklyn until 1898. Kings County is the most populous county in NY.
county_metadata.research_tips: Kings County (Brooklyn) has records split between the NYC Municipal Archives and the Kings County Clerk. The Municipal Archives at 31 Chambers Street holds birth records (1866+), death records (1847+), and marriage records (1866+). The Kings County Clerk has deed records from 1683 (among the oldest in the state). Brooklyn Historical Society (now Center for Brooklyn History at Brooklyn Public Library) has extraordinary genealogical collections including city directories from 1796, family manuscripts, and Brooklyn Eagle newspaper archive. For Dutch colonial families, the Dutch Reformed churches of Brooklyn (Flatbush, Flatlands, New Utrecht, Bushwick, Gravesend) have records from the 1650s published by Holland Society. Green-Wood Cemetery records (from 1838) document prominent families. Brooklyn's massive immigrant population is documented in Catholic parish records (Diocese of Brooklyn), synagogue records, and ethnic society archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1683 colonial era; Kings County Clerk

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from colonial era. Among oldest in NY.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent from 1683. Colonial Dutch records survive.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Oneida County in 1805. Black River valley in northern NY. Dairy farming dominant. Significant Mennonite and Amish communities. Relatively isolated terrain.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lewis County Clerk in Lowville holds deed records from 1805 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1805 records in Oneida County. Lewis County is heavily rural with dairy farming as the primary industry. The Lewis County Historical Society in Lowville has genealogical files and cemetery records. Significant Mennonite and Amish populations have their own record-keeping traditions — Croghan area Mennonite church records are important but may have restricted access. French Canadian immigration in the northern towns is documented in Catholic parish records (Diocese of Ogdensburg). The Black River Canal (1849-1924) connected the county to the Erie Canal system. Lumber industry records document the many sawmills that operated in the county. Lowville Academy records (founded 1808) have student rolls useful for identifying early families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1805; county clerk Lowville

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee and Ontario Counties in 1821. Genesee Valley in western NY. Named for Robert Livingston. SUNY Geneseo located here. Agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Livingston County Clerk in Geneseo holds deed records from 1821 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1821 records split between Genesee and Ontario Counties. The Livingston County Historian's office maintains genealogical files and cemetery transcriptions. The county was part of the Wadsworth family estate — James and William Wadsworth controlled vast landholdings and their tenant records at SUNY Geneseo Milne Library document hundreds of early settlers. The Genesee Valley Historical Society has family files. For Shakers, the Sodus Bay and Groveland Shaker communities generated communal records. SUNY Geneseo's Milne Library Special Collections has Wadsworth papers and local history. The county's fertile Genesee Valley attracted wheat farmers from New England. Dansville was a water cure resort whose patient records document visitors from across the nation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1821; county clerk Geneseo

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Chenango County in 1806. Central NY between Syracuse and Utica. Colgate University in Hamilton. Hop-growing region. Oneida Community utopian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Madison County Clerk in Wampsville holds deed records from 1806 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1806 records in Chenango County. The Oneida Community (1848-1881) utopian settlement in Oneida generated extraordinary communal records now at Syracuse University and Oneida Community Mansion House. The Madison County Historical Society in Oneida has genealogical files. Colgate University (Hamilton) archives have local history collections. The county's hop-growing industry (peak in 1870s-1880s) generated agricultural society and farm account records. Chittenango was birthplace of L. Frank Baum. The Stockbridge-Munsee settlement history in the Brothertown-Stockbridge area documents Native American displacement and is recorded at the Wisconsin Historical Society where the tribe relocated. Cazenovia was a planned settlement with well-documented records at Lorenzo State Historic Site.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1806; county clerk Wampsville
